title: Cross-Session Inventory Transfer
focus_questions: Did the new session inherit control from the prior session or begin correcting it?
Was the prior-session zone still active on first handoff interaction?

summary.thesis: A new session often inherits unfinished control, trapped inventory, or active zones from the prior session rather than starting from zero.
summary.operator_translation: Do not read the new session in isolation when the prior-session build is still close and active.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when the replay spans session handoff and prior-session zones remain relevant at the new open or first active rotation.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: one session hands off unfinished inventory or control into the next. Required evidence: a prior-session build or level near price, plus first handoff interaction showing inheritance or correction. Do not trade: if the new session opens too far away for prior inventory to matter or before handoff acceptance is known. Real trigger: first successful defense, continuation, or accepted correction through the inherited zone. Failure point: the prior-session zone proves irrelevant on first real interaction.

when_relevant: A prior session built a meaningful level, campaign, or trapped-inventory area.
The next session opens close enough for that prior work to matter.
The operator needs to know whether the new session inherits or corrects the old session's inventory.
required_evidence: a prior-session level or build is still near current price
the new session interacts with that inherited zone early enough for handoff logic to matter
acceptance or rejection on the first handoff interaction can be reviewed
confirmation_signals: the new session opens close enough to the inherited zone for it to matter immediately
the first meaningful interaction proves whether the prior session side still controls the level
continuation or correction after the handoff is supported by real acceptance, not just the first burst
invalidation_signals: the new session opens too far away for prior-session inventory to matter
the inherited zone produces no outcome on first real interaction
the supposed handoff read ignores a complete session reset in location and context
no_trade_conditions: Do not assume the new session starts from zero when prior-session inventory is still nearby and active.
Do not force prior-session logic when the new session opens too far away for that inventory to matter.
Do not call inheritance or correction before the first meaningful handoff interaction shows acceptance or rejection.

entry_archetypes.name: inherited defense
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after the new session proves it is defending and using the prior-session level rather than ignoring it.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if defense is only visual and not yet producing outcome.

entry_archetypes.name: accepted correction through inherited zone
entry_archetypes.description: Enter with correction after the new session accepts through the prior-session zone and invalidates inherited control.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the move is only testing through with no acceptance.

management_notes: Session handoff is strongest when the open occurs near a prior-session build, suppressor, or launchpad.
Inherited control is more credible after first defense or first accepted continuation, not before.
If the handoff fails quickly, prior-session work can become correction fuel rather than continuation support.
review_questions: What exactly did the prior session hand off into the new one: control, trapped inventory, or only a visible level?
Did the new session inherit and use that inventory, or immediately start correcting it?
Was the trade opened before the session handoff actually showed who was in control?
